PRJ_01 · 工程美学 · 2025

VitaTrail

A thermally regulated trail shoe engineered around female biomechanics — integrating liquid-cooling, scent modulation, and dual-skin waterproofing into a single high-performance system.

Context

Year 1 Design Engineering
Principles · Personal Project

Role

Sole Designer
Researcher · Prototyper

Tech Stack

Illustrator InDesign Photoshop Vizcom

Outcome

Concept Design
Appearance & Structure Prototype

01 · The Challenge

Designed for
the wrong body.

The women's outdoor footwear market exceeded $15 billion in 2025, representing over 40% of the total outdoor market — yet the vast majority of trail shoes on the shelf are men's lasts scaled down, not redesigned.

Female biomechanics differ structurally: a wider Q-angle at the knee drives pronation patterns that standard insole geometries ignore. Pressure distribution during trail running concentrates differently across the metatarsal and heel zones — data that most footwear manufacturers have never formally integrated into their design process.

Beyond mechanics, thermal regulation in existing outdoor shoes remains passive. On extended high-output trails, rising foot temperature degrades proprioception and accelerates fatigue — a problem that scales with duration. VitaTrail was scoped to address all three failure modes simultaneously.

Female foot pressure map · plantar distribution

Q-angle comparison · Female vs Male skeletal geometry

02 · The Exploration

15 ideas.
3 systems.

The ideation phase drew from four territories simultaneously: animal biomechanics (grip and torsion patterns from feline and avian anatomy), women's fashion (material language and silhouette logic), high-performance outdoor protection, and cross-industry technology transfer in footwear.

Fifteen divergent concepts were sketched and stress-tested against the biomechanical brief. Through convergence sessions, three core systems emerged as structurally compatible and collectively sufficient to close all identified performance gaps.

I

Biomimetic Anti-Slip Torsion Sole

Feline & avian grip morphology applied to multi-zone outsole geometry

II

Liquid-Cooling & Scent Circulation System

Pressure-driven coolant loop embedded in upper; passive scent release via heel compression

III

Dual-Skin Lightweight Waterproof Upper

Gore-Tex membrane + polymer outer with integrated micro-channel ventilation

Bionic sole study · animal grip morphology

System sketch · cooling loop & scent module

Upper system sketch · waterproof layer study

03 · The Prototype

Made to be
worn, not
displayed.

Two prototypes were fabricated: a lo-fi appearance prototype and a structural prototype — both built from accessible materials including foam board, corrugated cardboard, and plastic film.

Fabrication techniques spanned hot and cold cutting, adhesive bonding, and mechanical fastening with locking mechanisms — prioritising the replication of structural logic over surface finish.

Both prototypes were physically worn to validate wearability, last geometry, and heel-to-toe transition. The structural prototype confirmed the torsional stiffness of the carbon-analogue shank insert and the viability of the dual-skin lamination sequence under load.

05 · The Engineering

Three systems.
One shoe.

System I · Scent & Liquid Cooling

Walking pressure compresses a perfume-coolant reservoir embedded at the plantar surface. The compression simultaneously releases a controlled scent mist through micro-nozzles and drives liquid through a closed-loop micro-channel network woven into the upper. As the fluid circulates and returns to the reservoir it dissipates heat extracted from the foot — passive cooling that requires zero electronics and zero external power.

Key mechanism

CoolantPerfume-fluid
Drive forceHeel compression
Channel loc.Upper mid-layer
PowerZero (passive)
SecondaryScent diffusion

Layer sequence (outer → inner)

Transparent polymer outer shell
Embedded micro-channel coolant lines
Gore-Tex waterproof membrane
Breathable mesh lining

Waterproof

20,000 mmH₂O

Breathable

640 g/24h

System II · Dual-Skin Waterproof Upper

The upper is constructed as a four-layer laminate. The outermost transparent polymer shell provides structural rigidity and defines the shoe's visual language. Beneath it, the coolant micro-channels run laterally across the vamp and quarter. The third layer is a Gore-Tex membrane — 20,000 mmH₂O hydrostatic resistance with a vapour transmission rate of 640 g/24h — and the inner lining is a breathable mesh that completes the moisture management circuit.

System III · Biomimetic Anti-Torsion Sole

The sole stack is a three-tier composite. The uppermost carbon fibre shank provides torsional rigidity calibrated to female Q-angle mechanics — preventing medial collapse during pronated trail strikes. The middle layer is an Ultraboost-grade EVA foam cell cushion for energy return. The outsole is zoned into distinct lugging patterns derived from feline and avian grip morphologies: forefoot nodes for lateral engagement, mid-foot flex grooves, and heel macro-lugs for braking on loose terrain.

Sole stack (top → bottom)

Carbon fibre anti-torsion shankQ-angle corrected · lateral stability
Ultraboost EVA cushionEnergy return · impact absorption
Multi-zone biomimetic outsoleGrip · braking · flex zoned by region

Target weight

130 g
